patch.value:
Root cause: resolveLanceDbStore (src/core/search/vector-stores.ts) returned the raw settings path; consumers call resolve(storePath) which is cwd-dependent. pm-root discovery walks ancestors so item data stays correct, but the vector store forks.

Fix (working tree, session 2026-07-06): anchor relative lancedb paths to the workspace root derived from pmRoot (basename-based, separator-safe: <ws>/.agents/pm -> <ws>; bare --path roots anchor to themselves). resolveVectorStores(settings, pmRoot?) gained an optional backward-compatible param; call sites in search.ts, cache.ts (semantic refresh context) updated; health.ts buildVectorizationRuntimeSnapshot still needs pmRoot threading. Also remove the stray nested .agents/pm/.agents/ directory from the workspace.

Acceptance: pm search/reindex/health run from a workspace subdirectory never create .agents/pm/.agents/**; unit test covers anchorLanceDbStorePath for conventional root, bare root, and absolute path.
Relative lancedb vector-store path resolves against process cwd, creating nested .agents/pm/.agents/pm stores

Settings store a workspace-relative lancedb path (default .agents/pm/search/lancedb/). getLanceDbSnapshotPath/resolveLanceDbStore resolved it with path.resolve() against process cwd, so any pm invocation from a workspace subdirectory (including detached background-refresh children that inherit the parent cwd) silently created a second vector store under that subdirectory. Observed artifact: .agents/pm/.agents/pm/search/lancedb/.pm-cli-local-vectors/pm_items.json created 2026-07-06T08:22:48Z (matches vectorization-status.json generated_at), i.e. a refresh ran with cwd=<repo>/.agents/pm.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Fix implemented and verified 2026-07-06 - anchorLanceDbStorePath in vector-stores.ts anchors relative paths to the workspace root (basename-based pm-root derivation, separator-safe); resolveVectorStores(settings, pmRoot?) optional param threaded through search.ts, health.ts (buildVectorizationRuntimeSnapshot), reindex.ts (assertSemanticRuntimeAvailable), cache.ts semantic-refresh context. cache.ts isSemanticRefreshActive left unanchored on purpose (boolean availability check only). 4 regression tests added to search-provider-store-selection.spec.ts (conventional root, bare root, absolute path, no-root back-compat); spec passes 20/20. Stray .agents/pm/.agents/ directory removed from the workspace. tsc clean.

patch.value: pm search/reindex/health run from a workspace subdirectory never create .agents/pm/.agents/**; regression tests cover conventional root, bare --path root, absolute path, and no-root back-compat

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Evidence: implemented and verified relative LanceDB path anchoring through pmRoot-aware resolveVectorStores call sites in search, health, reindex, and semantic refresh. Focused linked test passed; temp-dir smoke from inside project/.agents/pm confirmed active LanceDB path anchored to project/.agents/pm/search/lancedb and no nested .agents/pm/.agents was created. Gates passed: pnpm build, pnpm lint, pnpm contracts:check, pnpm security:scan, pnpm audit --audit-level moderate, pm health --check-only --summary --json ok, pm validate history_drift ok with pre-existing validate_resolution_missing_fields warning, full coverage 264 files / 5436 tests / 100% statements branches functions lines.

patch.value: Relative LanceDB paths now resolve against the pmRoot-derived workspace root across search, health, reindex, and mutation semantic refresh.
pm search/reindex/health from workspace subdirectories do not create .agents/pm/.agents vector stores; regression tests cover conventional root, bare root, absolute path, and back-compat.
Focused linked tests passed, temp-dir subdirectory smoke proved the anchored path and absence of nested .agents/pm/.agents, and full coverage passed 100/100/100/100.
Fixed relative LanceDB vector-store path anchoring and verified no nested workspace store is created

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Review follow-up: accepted Gemini's platform-independence suggestion by using path.resolve in the LanceDB path anchoring expectations. Focused tests passed after the change: semantic-defaults, search-provider-store-selection, and health-command (93/93), plus pnpm typecheck.
